page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a substantial 480 MB L3 cache, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This server includes 5669 GB of local disk storage and is Premium Storage capable. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix operations, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES, VAES, SHA_NI).

In benchmark testing, the D96lds_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Passmark CPU metrics, including encryption and physics tests. It achieves top-tier results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. However, its single-core stress-ng performance and Passmark memory score fall into the average tier, highlighting a tradeoff where multi-threaded parallel execution and cache-heavy operations are prioritized over raw single-threaded speed.

Given its high vCPU count, dense local storage, and low memory-to-core ratio, this server represents a cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, high-throughput compression tasks, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is a compute-focused server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 192.0 GB of memory and a substantial 5669 GB of local storage. The processor integrates advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX for matrix operations. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high throughput in Redis and database operations, though single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile makes the D96lds_v7 highly cost-efficient for parallel compute-bound tasks, multi-threaded database workloads, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference where high memory-per-core ratios are not required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-threaded and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute server featuring dense local storage and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing and high-throughput database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
192 GB of system memory paired with 5669 GB of local storage
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 for accelerated mathematical operations
Top-tier multi-threaded performance, memory bandwidth, and Redis throughput
Optimized for compute-heavy databases, web serving, and CPU-based inference
